findings.evidence: `FundamentalGroup.map_range_eq_bot_of_subsingleton` is private at lines 32-34; Mathlib gives only the implication from `[SimplyConnectedSpace X]` to `Subsingleton (FundamentalGroup X x)` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SimplyConnected.lean:69`.
findings.fix: Make the `[Subsingleton (FundamentalGroup A a₀)]` theorem public at the natural name, and derive the simply-connected convenience from it if desired.
findings.issue: `map_range_eq_bot_of_simplyConnected` exports only the special `[SimplyConnectedSpace A]` form, while the proof uses the strictly weaker private theorem `map_range_eq_bot_of_subsingleton`.

findings.evidence: `FundamentalGroup.map_range_le_mapOfEq_range_of_subsingleton` is private at lines 51-55 and is exactly the weaker signature used to prove the public theorem at lines 61-65.
findings.fix: Expose the existing `[Subsingleton (FundamentalGroup A a₀)]` range-inclusion theorem publicly, and derive this simply-connected version from it.
findings.issue: `map_range_le_mapOfEq_range_of_simplyConnected` also exports only the simply-connected special case, although the range inclusion needs only triviality of the fundamental group at `a₀`.

summary: The exported fundamental-group lemmas are stated with stronger `SimplyConnectedSpace` hypotheses even though the file already proves the natural basepoint-level `Subsingleton (FundamentalGroup A a₀)` versions privately.
